title: Gate announce-derived NETWORK location fallback behind explicit policy
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2.9.2 decides that peer announce coordinates are peer-owned routing metadata and must not become this node's local NETWORK location by default. Current no-hardware gateway_network_location_submit_announce() test boundary publishes the freshest announce coordinate as NETWORK source_name=mesh-announce, but production raw announce dispatch is not wired yet. What needs to be done: add an explicit Kconfig/runtime policy gate for approximate mesh-derived location fallback before production L2 announce dispatch can feed gateway_announce_ingest_frame()/gateway_announce_ingest_verified(); default the fallback off unless a gateway profile intentionally enables it; preserve source_class=NETWORK/source_name provenance, stale withdrawal, and lower priority than fresh manual/static or local-client sources; add native_sim/qemu tests for disabled-by-default routing-only retention and enabled approximate fallback. This must be complete before project-LICHEN-9c0g wires production L2 raw announce dispatch.
notes:
Review escalation from project-LICHEN-zte2.9.2: Goodall the 9th classified this as P1 because the new privacy semantics require an explicit local/build policy before peer announce coordinates can become a local NETWORK location, while current gateway code compiles announce_ingest.c/network_location.c unconditionally and gateway_announce_ingest_verified() forwards verified app_data to gateway_network_location_submit_announce(), which publishes source_name=mesh-announce. This Bead is a production blocker and blocks project-LICHEN-9c0g. Do not wire production L2 raw announce dispatch until this policy gate and disabled-by-default tests exist.
Implementation evidence: added CONFIG_LICHEN_GATEWAY_ANNOUNCE_NETWORK_LOCATION_FALLBACK default n. gateway_network_location_submit_announce() now stores verified announce coordinates for routing/diagnostic lookup but returns before publishing them as local NETWORK location unless the policy is enabled. Added ztest-only gateway_network_location_announce_test_set_fallback_enabled() so existing opt-in behavior remains covered while production builds use only Kconfig. Added regression test proving default-disabled policy keeps routing records but leaves HAL local location without lat/lon. Reviews: Feynman the 9th and Turing the 9th found no P0/P1/P2 findings. Validation: local git diff --check passed. Local west build -b native_sim configured then stopped at expected macOS POSIX architecture limitation. EC2 us-east-2a instance i-0a3288920ad0ba530, EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58, clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-ua1r. Command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-ua1r --extra-args=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-ua1r/lichen. Result: 2/4 selected configs passed, 2 static skips, 0 failed, 0 errored, 160 test cases executed, 0 warnings, 66.09s.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware fallback gate. Added default-off CONFIG_LICHEN_GATEWAY_ANNOUNCE_NETWORK_LOCATION_FALLBACK, preserved announce coordinate retention for routing/diagnostics while preventing default local NETWORK publication, added ztest opt-in hook and disabled-by-default regression coverage, completed two clean independent reviews, and validated with EC2 native_sim/qemu Twister.
